**Hui Liangyu** ([Chinese](/wiki/Chinese_language "Chinese language"): 回良玉; [pinyin](/wiki/Pinyin "Pinyin"): *Huí Liángyù*, [Xiao'erjing](/wiki/Xiao%27erjing "Xiao'erjing"): ﺧُﻮِ ﻟِﯿْﺎ ﻳُﻮْْ; born October 1944) is a Chinese retired politician who served as a [vice premier of China](/wiki/Vice_Premier_of_China "Vice Premier of China") from 2003 to 2013, in charge of [agriculture](/wiki/Agriculture_in_China "Agriculture in China").[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-hlysh-2)

He previously served as the [party secretary](/wiki/Chinese_Communist_Party_Committee_Secretary "Chinese Communist Party Committee Secretary") of [Anhui](/wiki/Anhui "Anhui") from 1998 to 1999, and the party secretary of [Jiangsu](/wiki/Jiangsu "Jiangsu") from 1999 to 2002.[[2]](#cite_note-hlysh-2)

## Biography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hui_Liangyu&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biography")]

Hui was born in [Yushu](/wiki/Yushu,_Jilin "Yushu, Jilin"), [Jilin](/wiki/Jilin "Jilin") in October 1944.[[2]](#cite_note-hlysh-2) He is a member of the [Hui ethnic minority](/wiki/Hui_people "Hui people").[[2]](#cite_note-hlysh-2) Starting in 1969, he worked in a number of [Chinese Communist Party](/wiki/Chinese_Communist_Party "Chinese Communist Party") and government positions, rising to full membership in the [Politburo](/wiki/Politburo_of_the_Chinese_Communist_Party "Politburo of the Chinese Communist Party") of the [CCP Central Committee](/wiki/Central_Committee_of_the_Chinese_Communist_Party "Central Committee of the Chinese Communist Party") in November 2002.[[2]](#cite_note-hlysh-2) He was the [CCP party chief](/wiki/Chinese_Communist_Party_Committee_Secretary "Chinese Communist Party Committee Secretary") in [Jiangsu](/wiki/Jiangsu "Jiangsu") from 2000 to 2002.[[2]](#cite_note-hlysh-2) He served as a Vice Premier from 2003 to 2013.[[2]](#cite_note-hlysh-2)
